pending.body:
finding_summary: `terminal_event_for_attempt` regressed the attempt-boundary calculation for resumed runs. In `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:129-166` it prefers the last durable `run_started`/`run_resumed` boundary and only falls back to the inferred `started_at` boundary when no durable boundary exists. That loses the fail-closed behavior still preserved by `terminal_status_for_running_attempt` in `src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:200-206`. `sync_terminal_milestone_task_with_options` can now feed a newer lineage `started_at` into this helper (`src/cli/run.rs:1174-1185`, `src/cli/run.rs:1275-1317`) and still match an older `run_failed` from the same `run_id` if the resumed attempt crashed before `run_resumed` was durably written. In that window, CLI sync will reconcile the stale failure against the new retry and consume a retry / move the controller for the wrong attempt.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/project_run_record/queries.rs:129-166
- src/cli/run.rs:1174-1185
- src/cli/run.rs:1275-1317
remediation_hint: compute the lower boundary as `max(durable_boundary, inferred_boundary)` for `terminal_event_for_attempt`/`terminal_status_for_attempt`, and add a regression test for a resumed attempt with no durable `run_resumed` event.

finding_summary: retry escalation is driven by all known attempt starts, not by prior failed outcomes. `failure_attempt_number` assigns the current ordinal from `known_attempts(...)` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:747-824`), and `known_attempts` includes every `BeadStarted` journal entry and every lineage row regardless of outcome (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:1058-1084`). That ordinal is then used directly by `failure_outcome` to decide retryable vs `NeedsOperator` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:827-840`). A stray running/start-only attempt left behind by a crash or partial write therefore burns a retry even though no terminal `Failed` outcome was ever recorded, which contradicts the stated contract to count prior failed outcomes. A couple of orphaned starts can escalate the very first real failure directly to `NeedsOperator`.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:747-824
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:827-840
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:1058-1084
remediation_hint: base the retry counter on terminal failed attempts only; use started/running artifacts only to identify the exact attempt being replayed or to repair missing failure records, not to increment the retry count by themselves.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 13 -> Plan and Implement
